name: Pheasant with Grapes & White Wine Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Melt butter in a casserole or Dutch oven.
2. When hot, sauté pheasant gently all over until golden brown. Remove the bird.
3. Add flour, stock, and wine; blend smoothly. Bring to boil.
4. Add seasoning.
5. Return the bird to the casserole and cover. Cook in a 350°F oven for 35-45 minutes, or until the pheasant is tender. Turn the dish during cooking.
6. Peel grapes by dipping them in boiling water for a few seconds, then in cold water.
7. If not seedless, remove seeds from the grapes. Cover with a little lemon juice to prevent browning.
8. When the bird is tender, remove it and carve. Place on a serving dish and keep warm.
9. Add grapes to the sauce and cook for a couple of minutes. Season to taste and spoon over the pheasant. Serve with mashed potatoes and peas or spinach.
